categories.description: Repeated generation from the same inputs must produce no content diff after volatile paths and timestamps are normalized.
New generated files, fields, handlers, comments, or metadata are allowed only when existing public names and runtime behavior remain compatible.
Whitespace, gofmt, import ordering, or generated comment normalization may be accepted when no semantic token changes are present.
Deleted files, renamed symbols, changed JSON/OpenAPI/proto fields, removed config keys, or incompatible plugin protocol changes must block release until explained or migrated.

goctlGeneratorCompatibility.integrationPolicy: Generated upgrade dry-runs must fail when goctl-compatible profiles, flags, API tooling, route layout, or generated dependency boundaries drift.
goctlGeneratorCompatibility.rollbackOrEscalation: Pin the previous generator version or fall back to the default gofly-ai profile when goctl-compatible scaffold evidence, flags, API tooling, or route layout contracts fail.

steps.expectedOutput: historical and current fixtures compile and identify the profile used by the project
steps.rollbackOrEscalation: pin the current generator version until fixture compatibility and profile trust evidence are restored

steps.expectedOutput: repeat generation is deterministic and diff categories are recorded for old, current, and future profiles
steps.rollbackOrEscalation: discard generated output, keep the previous snapshot, and block upgrade until deterministic-repeat-generation passes

steps.expectedOutput: gozero-compatible profile, goctl-style flags, API tooling, route layout, and generated dependency boundaries stay compatible with upgrade dry-run fixtures
steps.rollbackOrEscalation: pin the previous generator or fall back to gofly-ai scaffolds until goctl-compatible evidence and generated dry-run reports converge

steps.expectedOutput: root dependencies, generated-project dependencies, toolchain dependencies, and integration dependencies keep ownership boundaries
steps.rollbackOrEscalation: revert or pin the dependency when ownership evidence, vulnerability scanning, or integration delegation fails

steps.expectedOutput: release evidence consumption and readiness score identify the artifacts an adopter must inspect before upgrade
steps.rollbackOrEscalation: block release promotion when readiness is not ready or required release evidence is missing

steps.expectedOutput: adoption risk class, promotion gate, and rollback or escalation action are available for each upgraded surface
steps.rollbackOrEscalation: treat the surface as candidate or rollback-required until the promotion gate passes locally

adopterUpgradeProof.policy: Each adopter upgrade path must name the fixture profile, expected diff class, repeat generation requirement, generated project smoke, dependency boundary, and rollback action before generated output is promoted.

paths.adopterDecision: Upgrade legacy generated services only when additive shims compile and repeat generation stays clean.
paths.dependencyBoundary: generated project go.mod or isolated temporary test module
paths.rollbackAction: Pin the previous generator and keep the v0.1 generated snapshot serving until compatibility shims compile.

paths.adopterDecision: Regenerate current services only when the diff is empty or formatting-only and generated smoke stays green.
paths.dependencyBoundary: generated project go.mod or isolated temporary test module
paths.rollbackAction: Discard the regeneration diff and keep the current generated project when repeat generation or smoke fails.

paths.adopterDecision: Use future fixtures only as tolerant preview evidence until experimental fields are promoted by a later release.
paths.dependencyBoundary: generated project go.mod or isolated temporary test module
paths.rollbackAction: Disable the future profile and pin current generation when experimental fields become breaking candidates.

executionContract.generatorCommand: go run ./cmd/gofly new service orders --module example.com/orders --dir <tmp>/<profile>/first/orders --config <copied service-config.json> --api <fixture.api> --proto <fixture.proto> --json
executionContract.repeatGeneration: generate the same profile twice into first and second directories and require diff -ru to be clean
executionContract.artifactPolicy: temporary generated projects and reports are runtime evidence only and must not be committed

profiles.rollbackOrEscalation: Pin the previous generator and keep the v0.1 generated snapshot serving until additive compatibility shims compile and repeat generation stays clean.
Discard the regeneration output and keep the current generated project when repeat generation, generated smoke, or diff classification produces unexplained drift.
Treat future fixtures as preview-only evidence and pin current generation when unsupported future fields become breaking candidates.

diffExplanationPolicy.blockingRule: Any generated diff that is not a clean repeat-generation result or an explained category with a rollback note must block release promotion.
diffExplanationPolicy.reportUse: The gofly.generated_version_compat_report.v1 generated version compatibility report is the machine-readable source for generated file count, go test status, repeat diff status, and expected diff explanation.

fidelityRows.rollbackOrEscalation: Fallback to the default gofly-ai profile or pin the previous generator when gozero-compatible layered files drift.
Keep goctl-style flags accepted with no-op warnings instead of changing positional parsing semantics.
Pin the previous generated API fixture and reject importer promotion when .api import handling changes route, field, or diff semantics.
Pin native protoc or the previous generated proto snapshot when imported proto types, package names, or generated service descriptors drift.

fidelityRows.surface: import aliases and generated symbol names
fidelityRows.rollbackOrEscalation: Pin the previous generator when include ordering, plugin aliases, Go package aliases, or generated symbol names collide after regeneration.
Discard generated output and keep the last known-good snapshot when repeat generation introduces unclassified diffs or missing rollback notes.

p10GoctlGeneratorFidelity.promotionPolicy: Generated scaffold promotion requires goctl-compatible profile evidence, accepted goctl-style flags, API import/diff/route fidelity, proto import compatibility, alias-collision checks, generated dependency boundaries, repeat diff classification, and rollback notes.
p10GoctlGeneratorFidelity.runtimeArtifactPolicy: Generated projects and dry-run reports remain volatile runtime evidence under .tmp-test or temporary directories and must not be committed.

executionContract.temporaryProjectRoot: GENERATED_VERSION_COMPAT_TMPDIR or mktemp -d under the local temp directory
executionContract.generatorCommand: go run ./cmd/gofly new service orders --module example.com/orders --dir <tmp>/<profile>/upgrade/orders --config <service-config.json> --api <orders.api> --proto <greeter.proto> --json
executionContract.moduleReplacement: go mod edit -replace github.com/imajinyun/gofly=<repo-root> inside the generated project only
executionContract.compileSmoke: go test ./... inside the generated project after go mod tidy
executionContract.repeatGeneration: generate the same profile twice and require clean normalized diff output before classifying adopter upgrade diffs
executionContract.reportSchema: emit gofly.generated_version_compat_report.v1 with generated file count, go test status, repeat diff status, and expected diff explanation
executionContract.artifactPolicy: temporary generated projects, diff reports, and smoke outputs are runtime evidence and must not be committed

profiles.upgradePath: legacy adopter regenerates a v0.1 service with the current gofly binary and reviews additive compatibility shims before rollout
profiles.dependencyBoundary: generated project go.mod or isolated temporary test module
profiles.rollbackAction: Pin the previous generator and keep the v0.1 generated snapshot serving if additive shims fail to compile or repeat generation drifts.

profiles.upgradePath: current adopter regenerates the service and accepts only empty, deterministic, or formatting-only diffs after generated smoke passes
profiles.dependencyBoundary: generated project go.mod or isolated temporary test module
profiles.rollbackAction: Discard the regenerated output and keep the current generated project when smoke, repeat generation, or diff classification produces unexplained drift.

profiles.upgradePath: future adopter uses the profile as preview-only evidence and validates that unsupported fields are ignored or classified before promotion
profiles.dependencyBoundary: generated project go.mod or isolated temporary test module
profiles.rollbackAction: Disable the future profile or pin current generation when experimental fields become breaking candidates or generated dependencies escape the generated module.

p11LiveUpgradeProof.promotionPolicy: Generated live upgrade promotion requires old, current, and future profiles to generate into temporary projects, compile with go test ./..., repeat cleanly, keep generated-only dependencies outside the root module, and provide rollback actions.
p11LiveUpgradeProof.runtimeArtifactPolicy: P11 live upgrade projects, reports, and diff outputs remain local runtime evidence under ignored temporary paths and must never be committed.

branchContract.source: real adopter branch or exported branch snapshot outside the gofly repository
branchContract.worktreePolicy: Replay must use a temporary worktree under GENERATED_VERSION_COMPAT_TMPDIR or the local temp directory and must not write generated runtime artifacts into the gofly repository.
branchContract.cleanupPolicy: Temporary worktrees, generated projects, branch replay reports, and diff outputs are runtime evidence only and must stay ignored.
branchContract.auditPolicy: Each replay record must name the adopter branch, base commit, fixture profile, generator version, generated snapshot source, normalized diff categories, smoke result, and rollback action before template promotion.

replaySteps.rollbackOrEscalation: Pin the previous generator and do not replay the branch when the baseline commit, generated snapshot, or profile cannot be traced.
Discard the temporary worktree and keep the branch on the previous generated snapshot when regeneration writes outside the replay root.
Treat every unclassified branch diff as a breaking-candidate until a migration note or rollback note explains the change.
Keep the adopter branch on the previous generated snapshot when branch smoke fails or generated-only dependencies escape the generated module.

profileMapping.branchUseCase: legacy go-zero style service branch replay with additive compatibility shims
profileMapping.rollbackAction: Keep the legacy branch on the previous generator and previous generated snapshot until additive shim smoke and repeat diff evidence pass.

profileMapping.branchUseCase: current service branch replay that should accept only deterministic or formatting-only generated diffs
profileMapping.rollbackAction: Discard the branch replay output and keep the current generated snapshot when any semantic or unclassified diff appears.

profileMapping.branchUseCase: preview branch replay for future tolerant fields before release promotion
profileMapping.rollbackAction: Disable future replay promotion and pin the current profile when branch output contains breaking candidates without migration notes.

p12RealBranchReplay.promotionPolicy: Generated template promotion requires a real branch replay record with traceable branch baseline, temporary worktree regeneration, classified repeat diff, generated project smoke, root dependency boundary, and rollback action.
p12RealBranchReplay.runtimeArtifactPolicy: P12 real branch worktrees, generated projects, reports, and diff outputs are local runtime evidence under ignored temporary paths and must never be committed.

executionPolicy.temporaryProjectSmoke: generated-version-compat-check must create temporary old, current, and future projects and run go test ./... inside each generated module
executionPolicy.repeatGeneration: generate each profile twice and require a clean diff -ru before any adopter-facing diff can be classified
executionPolicy.dependencyBoundary: generated project go.mod or isolated temporary test module
executionPolicy.artifactPolicy: temporary generated projects, branch replay worktrees, diff outputs, and generated smoke reports are runtime evidence and must not be committed

maturitySurfaces.surface: .api import, format, route, diff, and validation compatibility
maturitySurfaces.rollbackOrEscalation: Pin the previous generator and reject API importer promotion when .api import, format, route, diff, or validation semantics drift without migration notes.

maturitySurfaces.surface: old, current, and future generated compatibility fixtures
maturitySurfaces.rollbackOrEscalation: Pin the previous generator when old, current, or future fixtures stop generating, compiling, repeating cleanly, or reporting explainable diffs.
Discard temporary replay output and keep the adopter branch on the previous generated snapshot when branch replay lacks baseline, smoke, diff, or rollback evidence.

maturitySurfaces.surface: generated client contract for API adopters
maturitySurfaces.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep client generation pinned to the last passing language matrix when path, query, command, or language-specific generated output compatibility regresses.

maturitySurfaces.surface: repeat generation and generated upgrade diff classification
maturitySurfaces.rollbackOrEscalation: Treat every unclassified generated diff as a breaking candidate until a migration note, rollback note, or generator fix explains the output change.

maturitySurfaces.surface: generated project dependency ownership and root module hygiene
maturitySurfaces.rollbackOrEscalation: Reject generated scaffold promotion when generated-only dependencies escape into the gofly root module or temporary project dependency ownership cannot be explained.

p13GoctlGeneratorMaturity.promotionPolicy: P13 generator maturity requires .api import, .api format, .api validation, old/current/future fixtures, real adopter branch replay, multi-language client evidence, go test ./... generated project smoke, clean repeat-generation diff classification, and root module dependency hygiene before goctl-level maturity can be claimed.
p13GoctlGeneratorMaturity.runtimeArtifactPolicy: P13 generator maturity worktrees, generated projects, reports, and diff outputs are ignored runtime evidence under .tmp-test, GENERATED_VERSION_COMPAT_TMPDIR, or the local temp directory and must never be committed.

diffReport.rollbackNote: Keep the previous generated project snapshot and regenerate with the v0.1 fixture profile if additive compatibility shims fail to compile.
diffReport.summary: Legacy fixtures may receive additive generated files or shims, but repeated generation must remain deterministic.

generatedSnapshot.summary: Old contracts should regenerate with additive files and compatibility shims only.

dependencyPolicy.allowedLocation: generated project go.mod or isolated temporary test module
dependencyPolicy.rollbackOrEscalation: Keep the legacy generated project dependency set pinned inside the generated module until compatibility fixtures compile and root dependency policy passes.

diffReport.rollbackNote: Re-run the current profile with the last released gofly binary; any non-formatting diff must be treated as an upgrade blocker.
diffReport.summary: Current fixtures should produce no unexplained diff; formatting-only changes require explicit review evidence.

generatedSnapshot.summary: Current contracts should regenerate deterministically with no unexplained diff.

dependencyPolicy.allowedLocation: generated project go.mod or isolated temporary test module
dependencyPolicy.rollbackOrEscalation: Reject the upgrade diff and keep the current generated project dependency set when regeneration changes root module dependencies or fails repeat generation.

diffReport.rollbackNote: Disable future-only fixture fields or pin to the current profile when unsupported fields become breaking candidates.
diffReport.summary: Future fixtures may surface ignored or additive fields, but any deleted contract or changed generated public surface is a breaking candidate.

generatedSnapshot.summary: Future contracts should report explainable diffs and ignore unsupported fields safely.

dependencyPolicy.allowedLocation: generated project go.mod or isolated temporary test module
dependencyPolicy.rollbackOrEscalation: Disable the future-only profile or pin the current generator when experimental generated dependencies escape the generated module boundary.
